div.h2: Key Metrics for Nsight Compute Analysis

ol.li: Check DRAM Throughput → if > 75% of peak, likely memory-bound
Check FMA Utilization → if > 60%, likely compute-bound
Check Issue Slot Util → if < 30% with low other metrics, latency-bound
Check Stall Reasons → identifies specific bottleneck

div.p: Active warps / max warps per SM. Measures how well you're using SM resources to hide latency.
Good: 50-100% (memory-bound) Good: 75-100% (compute-bound) Bad: < 25%
• Reduce registers per thread • Reduce shared memory per block • Increase threads per block • Use --maxrregcount flag
Maximum possible occupancy based on launch config and kernel resource usage.
Gap from achieved: < 10% Bad: Large gap indicates runtime limits
If gap is large: • Check shared memory bank conflicts • Check for dynamic parallelism • Verify block count vs SM count
Number of 32-bit registers allocated per thread. Directly affects occupancy limits.
Good: < 64 registers Warning: 64-128 registers Bad: > 128 registers (spilling likely)
• Simplify expressions • Use __launch_bounds__ • Compile with --maxrregcount=N • Check for register spilling
Actual bytes/sec transferred between GPU and DRAM. The ultimate memory bandwidth metric.
Memory-bound target: > 80% of peak Good: > 600 GB/s (A100) Good: > 500 GB/s (RTX 3080) Bad: < 50% of peak
If low and memory-bound: • Check coalescing (load/store efficiency) • Use shared memory for reuse • Increase thread/data parallelism • Check for strided access patterns
Useful bytes loaded / total bytes fetched from DRAM. Measures coalescing effectiveness.
Good: > 80% Warning: 50-80% Bad: < 50% (non-coalesced)
• Ensure adjacent threads read adjacent addresses • Align data to 128-byte boundaries • Use SoA instead of AoS • Check stride patterns
Useful bytes stored / total bytes written to DRAM. Measures write coalescing.
Good: > 80% Warning: 50-80% Bad: < 50%
• Same as load efficiency • Ensure contiguous writes • Avoid scattered write patterns
Percentage of memory requests served from L2 cache. Indicates data reuse effectiveness.
Good: > 60% Warning: 30-60% Bad: < 30% (cache thrashing)
• Use shared memory for frequently accessed data • Tile/block your algorithm • Reduce working set size • Check for random access patterns
Average active threads per warp / 32. Measures branch divergence impact.
Good: > 90% Warning: 50-90% Bad: < 50% (severe divergence)
• Restructure branches for warp coherence • Use warp-level primitives • Sort data by branch outcome • Avoid data-dependent branching
Instructions issued / max possible instructions. Measures overall scheduler efficiency.
Compute-bound target: > 60% Warning: 30-60% Bad: < 30% (latency-bound)
• Increase occupancy • Check for synchronization bottlenecks • Verify enough warps per SM • Reduce __syncthreads() frequency
Cycles warps waited for memory operations. Indicates memory-bound kernel.
If dominant (> 40%): Memory-bound Action: Optimize memory access If low (< 10%): Not memory-limited
• Improve coalescing • Use shared memory • Increase occupancy for latency hiding • Reduce memory traffic
Cycles warps waited for execution pipe availability. Indicates compute-bound kernel.
If dominant (> 40%): Compute-bound Action: Reduce instruction count If low (< 10%): Not compute-limited
• Reduce arithmetic operations • Use tensor cores if applicable • Consider lower precision (FP16) • Fuse operations
Cycles warps waited at __syncthreads() barriers. Indicates synchronization overhead.
Good: < 10% Warning: 10-30% Bad: > 30% (excessive sync)
• Reduce __syncthreads() calls • Check for divergent paths crossing barriers • Verify all threads reach barriers • Consider warp-level sync instead
Cycles warps were ready but not chosen by scheduler. Indicates scheduler contention.
Good: < 20% Warning: 20-40% Bad: > 40% (too many warps)
• May actually need FEWER warps • Check if occupancy is too high • Verify workload balance across warps
Number of serialized accesses due to bank conflicts. Critical for shared memory performance.
Good: 0 conflicts (ideal) Warning: < 2× expected accesses Bad: > 4× expected accesses
• Add padding to shared memory arrays • Change access patterns • Use warp shuffle instead • Align data to avoid bank overlap
Bytes/sec transferred through shared memory. Should be high if using shared mem effectively.
Good: > 1 TB/s (effective use) Warning: 100 GB/s - 1 TB/s Bad: < 100 GB/s (underutilized)
• Increase data reuse in shared memory • Load larger tiles • Check for bank conflicts • Verify shared memory is actually used
Usage of fused multiply-add units. Primary compute throughput indicator.
Compute-bound target: > 70% Memory-bound: < 30% expected Latency-bound: < 30% with low mem
If compute-bound and low: • Check for data dependencies • Unroll loops • Increase arithmetic intensity • Use tensor cores for matrix ops
Usage of specialized matrix multiply units. Only relevant for matrix operations.
If using tensor cores: > 50% If not intended: 0% Bad: < 50% when intended
• Ensure matrix dimensions are multiples of 8/16 • Use WMMA or cuBLAS APIs • Check precision requirements • Verify tensor core is enabled
